landing.title: Plausible Analytics | Simple, privacy-friendly Google Analytics alternative
landing.text:
Plausible Analytics | Simple, privacy-friendly Google Analytics alternative
Why Plausible
Simple analytics dashboard
Get all the important stats on one single page. No training necessary.
Lightweight script
Loads fast and won't affect your page speed or Core Web Vitals. A fraction of the size of Google Analytics.
Privacy-friendly analytics
Built with visitor privacy in mind. No cookie banner required.
Open source
The code is public and auditable. Self-host on your own infrastructure or use our hosted version.
EU-hosted
Built and hosted in the EU. Your data never leaves European-owned infrastructure.
Who it's for
Ecommerce
SaaS
Agencies
Creators & publishers
White-label analytics
Enterprise
Compare
vs Google Analytics
Migrate from GA4
vs Matomo
vs Cloudflare Analytics
Resources
Documentation
Everything you need to get up and running with Plausible.
Blog
Stories about privacy, web analytics and building Plausible.
Tools
Free calculators and utilities to help you understand your analytics data.
Status
Check the live status and uptime of Plausible services.
Product updates
Major new features and improvements.

Why Plausible
- Simple analytics dashboard
- Lightweight script
- Privacy-friendly analytics
- Open source
- EU-hosted
Who it's for
- Ecommerce
- SaaS
- Agencies
- Creators & publishers
- White-label analytics
- Enterprise
Compare
- vs Google Analytics
- Migrate from GA4
- vs Matomo
- vs Cloudflare Analytics
Resources
- Documentation
- Blog
- Tools
- Status
- Product updates
Pricing
Easy to use and privacy-friendly
Google Analytics alternative
Plausible is powerful, lightweight analytics. No cookies, just insights. Made and hosted in the EU, powered by European-owned infrastructure. 🇪🇺
Start free trial
View live demo
Why use Plausible Analytics?
Google Analytics is overkill for most site owners. Plausible gives you clear, useful insights without complexity, training or prior analytics experience.
Automatic scroll depth tracking
Scroll depth from 1 to 100 percent is tracked automatically on every page. No setup, no tag managers. Create scroll depth goals without code.
Monitor traffic from AI tools
See which AI tools like ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Claude send you traffic. Identify pages that attract AI visitors and the conversions they complete.
Analyze SEO performance
Connect Search Console to see search queries and organic performance directly in your Plausible dashboard. Track SEO content and conversions in one place.
Measure paid ads and campaigns
Track UTM campaigns with automatic channel grouping for Paid Search or Affiliates. Analyze landing pages, conversions and locations.
Real-time dashboard
Monitor live traffic with the same metrics as your main dashboard. Updates every 30 seconds without refreshing the page.
Codeless goals and revenue tracking
Turn any page into a goal. Automatically track file downloads, form completions and external link clicks. Set up custom events and revenue tracking.
Funnels and user journeys
Measure drop-off across a fixed sequence of steps. Or explore the paths visitors actually take to discover flows you didn't predict.
Built-in bot filtering
Exclude bots, referrer spam and data center traffic so your stats reflect real human visits, out of the box.
People ❤️ Plausible
Plausible runs reliably at any scale, from small sites to hundreds of millions of monthly visitors, and is trusted by thousands of companies that have switched from Google Analytics.
Paying subscribers
18k
Tracked pageviews
260B
Uptime (Last 90 days)
99.99%
Clem Delangue
Co-founder and CEO at Hugging Face
We’re massive users of Plausible here at Hugging Face.
DHH
Co-founder and CTO at 37signals
Been a very happy customer of Plausible at Basecamp. Wonderful to see domains like web stats that were once a wasteland due to monopoly weight spring new, better options ❤️
John O'Nolan
Founder and CEO at Ghost
Check out Plausible if you haven't yet - fantastic product. We switched over everything from GA.
Cyrus Shepard
SEO consultant and former Moz lead
My 100% favorite GA4 alternative so far is Plausible. Not free and not a ton of bells and whistles, but SOOOO easy to use (for clients too) and the data is near real-time. A good solution for ~70% of websites struggling with GA4.
Rob Hope
Product designer and founder
Just replaced my full network's Google Analytics with Plausible - I can't remember when last I was this impressed by a SaaS UX + design 💯
Became a paying customer within 1hr of the 30-day trial.
Laura Roeder
Founder at Paperbell
I've been super happy with Plausible. All the conversion tracking we need, it has every report we use.
It's time to ditch Google Analytics
Google Analytics is frustrating to use, difficult to understand, slow to load and privacy-invasive. That's why we built Plausible Analytics, a simple, privacy-first alternative that doesn't cut corners.
Here's what makes Plausible a great
Google Analytics alternative
and why over 18,000 paying customers trust us with their website and business insights:
Simple analytics at a glance
Plausible is
simple analytics
. It is easy to understand and cuts through the noise. Check your site traffic and get the essential insights on one page in one minute. Get an aggregated view of all your sites in a consolidated dashboard. There are no layers of menus and no need to build custom reports, custom dashboards or PowerPoint documents. You can even import your Google Analytics stats.
Lightweight script that keeps your site speed fast
Plausible is
lightweight analytics
. Our script is 54 times smaller than Google Analytics. Every visitor that loads your site downloads 135KB less JavaScript. Less data transferred means less energy used and less carbon emitted, for every visitor, every day. A site with 100,000 monthly visitors saves around 4 kg of CO2 per year by switching.
No need for cookie banners or GDPR consent
Plausible is
privacy-friendly analytics
that doesn't process personal data or track individual users. No cookies, no persistent identifiers, no cross-site or cross-device tracking. Your site data is not used for any other purposes. All visitor data is exclusively processed on servers owned and operated by European companies and never leaves the EU.
We invite you to take a look around, explore our
live demo
and
try Plausible
for free. We'd be honored to have you as a customer. Thank you.
Uku and Marko
Co-founders
P.S. We're a completely independent, self-funded, bootstrapped and profitable team of 10, running since 2018. No outside investors, no acquisition targets. We choose the subscription business model rather than surveillance capitalism. Our code is open source too, so you're never locked in. Read more
about us
.
Traffic based plans that match your growth
Sign up for 30-day free trial. No credit card required.
2 months free

changelog.title: What’s New | Plausible Analytics
changelog.text:
What’s New | Plausible Analytics
Why Plausible
Simple analytics dashboard
Get all the important stats on one single page. No training necessary.
Lightweight script
Loads fast and won't affect your page speed or Core Web Vitals. A fraction of the size of Google Analytics.
Privacy-friendly analytics
Built with visitor privacy in mind. No cookie banner required.
Open source
The code is public and auditable. Self-host on your own infrastructure or use our hosted version.
EU-hosted
Built and hosted in the EU. Your data never leaves European-owned infrastructure.
Who it's for
Ecommerce
SaaS
Agencies
Creators & publishers
White-label analytics
Enterprise
Compare
vs Google Analytics
Migrate from GA4
vs Matomo
vs Cloudflare Analytics
Resources
Documentation
Everything you need to get up and running with Plausible.
Blog
Stories about privacy, web analytics and building Plausible.
Tools
Free calculators and utilities to help you understand your analytics data.
Status
Check the live status and uptime of Plausible services.
Product updates
Major new features and improvements.
Follow Plausible:
Twitter
,
Bluesky
,
Mastodon
and
LinkedIn
Pricing
Login
Start free trial
My dashboard
Start free trial
Log in
My dashboard
Why Plausible
- Simple analytics dashboard
- Lightweight script
- Privacy-friendly analytics
- Open source
- EU-hosted
Who it's for
- Ecommerce
- SaaS
- Agencies
- Creators & publishers
- White-label analytics
- Enterprise
Compare
- vs Google Analytics
- Migrate from GA4
- vs Matomo
- vs Cloudflare Analytics
Resources
- Documentation
- Blog
- Tools
- Status
- Product updates
Pricing
What's new in Plausible Analytics
Plausible is updated multiple times per week. Here we list the major new features and improvements. Take a look at our
feedback board
to see what's coming next. Follow us on
Twitter
,
Bluesky
,
Mastodon
and
LinkedIn
for more updates.
May 27, 2026
User journeys is here!
You can now see the actual paths visitors take through your site with the new User Journeys feature.
Analyze journeys from both a starting point and end point perspective to understand how visitors move between pages and events, discover the most common paths leading to conversions, uncover drop-off points and see conversion rates for each journey.
To get started, open the
Explore
tab from the report navigation bar alongside Goals, Properties and Funnels.
L﻿earn more here
.
Apr 14, 2026
Strict order funnels for precise path analysis
You can now make your funnels strict.
When enabled, every step must happen in exact consecutive order with no other actions in between.
This is useful when you want to measure a tightly defined path and ensure any detour disqualifies the conversion.
Learn more in
our funnel analysis docs
.
Mar 18, 2026
Sort your sites by traffic
I﻿f you have multiple sites on Plausible, you can now sort them by:
Most/least visitors in the last 24 hours
Domain name (A–Z or Z–A)
Pinned sites would still stay on top. Visit your
All Sites
page to try.
Feb 11, 2026
Last 24 Hours
A new time range for “Last 24 Hours” is now available on your dashboard. Open the time range picker to select it or press
H
on your keyboard to switch instantly and see how your site has performed over the past 24 hours.
Feb 4, 2026
Goals with custom properties
You can now select up to three existing custom properties and values directly in the goal settings. This lets you turn a broad goal into a more specific one, like a Purchase goal for specific subscription plans and payment cycles.
T﻿his works with all types of goals: pageview goals, custom events and scroll goals.
Goals with properties behave like built-in
segments
for your dashboard. These would also help make
funnels
more precise, since each step can now represent a specific version of a goal instead of a
catch all
.
P.S. Sending custom properties works exactly as before. You still send them in your goal snippet and can use them for filtering and breakdowns across the dashboard.
L﻿earn more about
goals
and
custom properties
.
Jan 5, 2026
Limited dashboard sharing
H﻿appy new year! Here are a few updates to kick off the new year. 🎈
Limited dashboard sharing
When
sharing your dashboard
with external parties, you can now choose to show only specific, filtered data, i.e.,
share only a segment of you stats
.
Anyone opening such a shared link will only see that segment and won’t be able to remove it. They would be able to add more filters to drill down further, but they won’t be able to see any data outside the selected segment.
This makes it easy to share only the data you want with the right audience.
O﻿ther updates
You can now see what percentage of your visitors came from different traffic sources, locations, and devices, as well as what percentage visited each page. A percentage column appears when you hover over a report and when the report is expanded.
Enterprise customers now have the option to access their privacy-friendly event level data with
scheduled raw event data exports
.
Nov 24, 2025
Revenue breakdown in your reports
I﻿f you track your
revenue goals
in Plausible, you can now see a breakdown of your revenue within the individual reports as well.
Whenever the revenue goal is applied on your dashboard and you expand any report (by clicking on “Details”), you will find a new “Revenue” column appear. This helps you see exactly which traffic sources, top/entry/exit pages, locations, browser/OS/device types, and custom properties contributed to how much revenue and make judgements on what drives value best.
Nov 17, 2025
Introducing, Consolidated Views
If you have multiple websites/dashboards in your Plausible account, you will find a brand new consolidated view on your
All Sites
page.
T﻿his gives you an overview of traffic across all your websites, allows you to quickly see the total number of visitors and other key metrics across all your properties in one place and works like any other dashboard that you can access and change the settings of. Find full info
here
.
Nov 5, 2025
Team owners can now enforce 2FA for extra security, and our security practices
W﻿e’ve made security in Plausible even stronger.
New
: Team owners can now make
two-factor authentication (2FA)
mandatory
for all members. This helps teams keep their accounts and data even more secure. You can manage your team’s 2FA settings in the Team Settings section. Everything you need to know is
here
.
Overview
: W﻿e take security very seriously, here’s a reminder of everything you can do to keep your account secure:
Manage active sessions:
View and
remotely log out
of any device from your account settings. All sessions automatically expire after 14 days of inactivity.
Password protection:
If you change your password, Plausible
automatically signs you out
of all other devices to keep your account safe.
Single Sign-On (SSO):
Teams on eligible plans can
enable SSO
to make logging in simpler and more secure.
Beyond account-level security, we follow strict best practices: we don’t use cookies, don’t collect personal data, and encrypt all data in transit and at rest. We’re also fully GDPR, CCPA, and PECR compliant. Learn more about how we keep your data safe
here
.
P.S. We’ve also made the dark theme even darker. If you prefer dark mode,
give it a try
!
Oct 8, 2025
Plug-and-play setup for optional measurements, automatic form analytics, and more
W﻿e have so many updates to share with you!
1. Brand new Plausible script for easier setup and more flexibility
The new Plausible script makes managing your analytics easier than ever. You can now enable or disable optional measurements (like outbound link clicks, file downloads, and form submissions) or even change your site’s domain —
all without changing your site snippet again
.
If you’re using the previous script, you’ll need to update to this new version once to take advantage of these improvements.
2. Introducing, automatic form submission tracking
Form submissions tracking
is now available as an optional measurement to track. Just toggle on to start tracking.
Plausible automatically detects and records all form submissions across your site, making it easy to measure signups, contact form conversions, etc.
3﻿. Official Google Tag Manager template
I﻿f you prefer installing/managing Plausible using Google Tag Manager, it’s now way easier to do that.
We’ve launched our
official GTM template
, allowing you to set up Plausible through Google Tag Manager with a simple, code-free configuration. Best of both worlds!
Jul 29, 2025
SSO is now available!
N﻿ow you can enable SSO for your team on Plausible. SSO gives you a way to access Plausible without having to create a separate account for Plausible and managing additional credentials.
This is possible by first logging in to identity provider (IdP) - be it Google, Okta, Microsoft (Entra) or other - and accessing Plausible through it.
Our SSO implementation is SAML 2.0 compliant and it should be compatible with almost every identity provider as SAML 2.0 protocol is widely supported.
L﻿earn more about the implementation on our
docs
,
contact us
for enabling this feature.
Jul 8, 2025
Introducing, Managed Proxy
I﻿f you want your Plausible script to bypass some ad blockers for more accurate stats, you can
proxy
the Plausible script and endpoint through your own domain name, making it a first-party connection.
W﻿hat’s new
: If you’d rather not deal with managing your own proxy, we can now handle it for you. Just set up a CNAME record using the instructions we’ll send you, update the snippet on your site, and we’ll take care of the rest.
Managed proxy is available on our Enterprise plans,
contact us
for details.
Apr 8, 2025
Improved "Time on Page" metric
We’ve made some big upgrades to how the “Time on Page” metric works:
M﻿ore accurate tracking:
Prior to 1 March 2025, the Time on Page was calculated as the difference between the point a visitor landed on a page and when they moved on to the next page. The new tracking method relies on engagement signals. This allows us to provide you with a more precise and reliable measure of user engagement which includes visitors who bounce away from your pages and which does not include time not spent actively on your pages.
View it on the top graph:
You can now click on the Time on Page metric to see how it trends over time (available for date ranges starting 1 March 2025).
Sort by it:
In the Top Pages Details view, you can now sort pages by Time on Page to quickly see where visitors spend the most (or least) time.
W﻿hen viewing data for periods before 1 March 2025, the legacy Time on Page metric is displayed along with a note in the tooltip.
Apr 1, 2025
Create a team in Plausible
It’s now much easier to manage teams on Plausible. You can now:
Add multiple owners and billing contacts, so you’re not stuck if someone’s away or has left the company.
Invite multiple people to a site at once.
Add someone to several sites in one go.
No more one-by-one invites.
You’ll find the new
“Create a Team”
option in the upper-right menu. You can also view and switch between your teams there — and still access your personal sites under
“My Personal Sites”
.
Team roles include: Viewer, Editor, Admin, Billing, and Owner.
Mar 10, 2025
Scroll Depth tracking
The scroll depth metric is now available in your Plausible Analytics dashboard. You can find it in the Top Pages report or in your dashboard’s top row when filtering by a page or pages.
W﻿e are tracking this metric automatically for all pages on your site—no setup required. We track scroll depth at all page heights––(1% to 100%).
Introducing, Scroll Depth goals
You can now set up Scroll Depth Goals to track how many visitors reach and exceed a specific scroll depth percentage on your selected pages. Learn more
here
.
Mar 6, 2025
Save and reuse segments
You can now save segments for quick and easy access! When you create a segment using one or more filters, you will find a new option to save it along with a display name (e.g.,
Organic signups
) and access it later from your “Filter” menu.
There are two types of segments you can create:
Personal Segments
– Save segments for your own use.
Site Segments
(business plan) – Share saved segments with your team members.
L﻿earn all details about using saved segments
here
.
Feb 5, 2025
Automatically track form completions with the WordPress plugin
We just released version 2.2.0 of the Plausible WordPress plugin. The highlight of this update is you can now track contact form submissions automatically, without any manual tagging or extra setup.
We have verified and tested this tracking with Contact Form 7, WP Forms, Ninja Forms and Elementor Forms but it should work for most other forms plugins as well.
To enable form tracking:
Go to the
“Enhanced measurements”
section in the Plausible WordPress plugin settings.
Toggle on
“Form completions”
tracking.
Once enabled, all successful form completions will be tracked and displayed in the
“Goal Conversions”
report in your Plausible Analytics dashboard.
You’ll see a
“WP Form Completions”
goal appear once the first successful submission is recorded. If you have multiple forms on your site, you can click on this goal to see a breakdown by page.
T﻿o learn more about the Plausible WordPress plugin, visit
our guide
.
Jan 23, 2025
Exclude goal conversions
We’ve added a new filter option to the dashboard when analyzing by goals. In addition to the existing “is” and “contains” operators, you can now use the “
is not
” operator.
This enhancement allows you to segment and analyze those sessions in which visitors didn’t convert on specific goal(s).
Jan 21, 2025
Plausible Analytics Looker Studio Connector is live!
The official Plausible Analytics Looker Studio Connector is now available!
Easily fetch your Plausible Analytics data using the Stats API and create custom, dynamic reports directly in Google Looker Studio.
Find the official connector
here
. Get started with our
setup guide
.
Nov 19, 2024
Introducing Top Channels Report
You can now

Plausible Analytics documentation
Plausible Analytics
is a simple, privacy-friendly alternative to Google Analytics. No cookies, no personal data collection, no consent banners required. One lightweight script, one easy-to-read dashboard. Running since 2018 and used by 18,000+ teams. Explore the
live demo
to see what it looks like.

Get Started
Integration guides
On this page
Integration guides: WordPress, Shopify, Next.js and more
Installing Plausible on a website can be a slightly different process depending on what tools you're using to manage your site.
Don't have a Plausible account yet? Create one first, then follow the guide for your platform
Create free account
We've put together several guides that cover popular website builders and content management systems (CMS). These can help you set up and start counting your site visitors in no time. Thanks to the members of our community, there are also third-party integrations and plugins for various frameworks.
Official integrations
​
These are built and maintained by the Plausible team. Unlike community plugins, official integrations stay current as the product evolves and are supported directly by us.
Add the snippet to the header of your site
​
To integrate your website with Plausible, you can manually insert our tracking snippet into the header section of your site. See
the details here
.
WordPress plugin
​
The
official WordPress plugin
handles installation, event tracking and dashboard access without any code. Built specifically for WordPress: supports WooCommerce revenue tracking, automatic form tracking, author and category stats, proxy mode and more. If your site runs on WordPress, this is the recommended setup.
Google Tag Manager template
​
The
official GTM template
is available in the Google Tag Manager community gallery. Install Plausible and configure event tracking from the GTM dashboard with no code changes. Works well for agencies managing multiple sites from a single GTM account.
Looker Studio connector
​
The
official Looker Studio connector
connects your Plausible data to Google's free data visualization tool. Build custom dashboards, branded client reports and views that blend Plausible data with other sources like ad spend or CRM data. Available on the
Business plan
.
NPM package
​
The
official NPM package
lets you add Plausible to any JavaScript project via your package manager. Built and maintained by the Plausible team. Useful for React, Vue, Next.js and other framework-based setups where installing via a script tag is impractical.
Run Plausible as a first-party connection
​
You can run Plausible script as a first-party connection from your domain name in order to bypass adblockers.
Learn more here
.
Server side tracking and mobile apps tracking
​
Our events API can be used to record pageviews and custom events server side without the use of JavaScript. You can use the events API to count users on your Android and/or iOS mobile apps too.
Learn more here
Community contributed integrations
​
The integrations below are built and maintained by third parties in the open source community.
While we’re grateful for the contributions, we do not develop, audit or maintain these plugins, and we do not guarantee their compatibility. They may become outdated or stop working as platforms or Plausible evolve.
For a fully supported setup, we recommend using our official script or one of the official integrations listed above.
Website builders and CMS platforms
​
Step-by-step guides for popular website builders and content management systems.

​
Platform

Community node for working with the Plausible API

Add Plausible records to Prometheus with the Plausible exporter
Data pipelines, warehouses and other ways of viewing Plausible stats elsewhere
​
See this list
for data pipelines, warehouses, MCP servers and other ways of viewing Plausible stats elsewhere. For instance, our
stats API
,
official WordPress plugin
and
official Google Looker Studio connector
.
Let us know about any other integrations that you've built and we will feature them in this list. Thanks for your support!
Last updated
on
May 29, 2026

analysis.name: Plausible
analysis.tagline: Easy to use and privacy-friendly Google Analytics alternative
analysis.value_proposition: A lightweight, cookieless web analytics tool that surfaces all essential site insights on one dashboard without consent banners, built and hosted in the EU.
analysis.ideal_customer: Small-to-mid-size website owners, SaaS teams, agencies, and ecommerce stores wanting simple privacy-compliant analytics without Google Analytics complexity

features.name: single-page analytics dashboard
features.description: All essential site stats on one page with no sub-menus, custom reports, or training required
features.evidence_quote: Get all the important stats on one single page. No training necessary.

features.name: real-time dashboard
features.description: Live view of current visitors updating every 30 seconds without page refresh
features.evidence_quote: Monitor live traffic with the same metrics as your main dashboard. Updates every 30 seconds

features.name: scroll depth tracking
features.description: Automatic scroll depth 1–100% on every page with no setup, tag managers, or code
features.evidence_quote: Scroll depth from 1 to 100 percent is tracked automatically on every page. No setup, no tag managers.

features.name: ai traffic monitoring
features.description: Track referral traffic from AI tools like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Claude
features.evidence_quote: See which AI tools like ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Claude send you traffic.

features.name: google search console integration
features.description: See search queries and organic performance directly in the Plausible dashboard
features.evidence_quote: Connect Search Console to see search queries and organic performance directly in your Plausible dashboard.

features.name: utm campaign tracking
features.description: Track UTM campaigns with automatic channel grouping for Paid Search, Affiliates, and more
features.evidence_quote: Track UTM campaigns with automatic channel grouping for Paid Search or Affiliates.

features.name: goal and conversion tracking
features.description: Set up pageview goals, custom events, scroll goals, file downloads, form completions, outbound link clicks without code
features.evidence_quote: Turn any page into a goal. Automatically track file downloads, form completions and external link clicks.

features.name: revenue tracking
features.description: Attribute ecommerce revenue to traffic sources, pages, locations, and devices
features.evidence_quote: Ecommerce revenue tracking: attribute revenue to traffic sources

features.name: revenue breakdown in reports
features.description: Revenue column appears in expanded reports per traffic source, page, location, device when revenue goal is active
features.evidence_quote: you can now see a breakdown of your revenue within the individual reports as well

features.name: funnel analysis
features.description: Measure drop-off across a defined sequence of steps; supports optional strict ordering
features.evidence_quote: Funnel analysis: measure drop-off across a defined sequence of steps

features.name: strict order funnels
features.description: Enforce exact consecutive step ordering in funnels so any detour disqualifies conversion
features.evidence_quote: When enabled, every step must happen in exact consecutive order with no other actions in between.

features.name: user journeys
features.description: Explore actual paths visitors take from start and end points; discover common conversion paths and drop-offs
features.evidence_quote: Analyze journeys from both a starting point and end point perspective to understand how visitors move

features.name: custom properties
features.description: Attach metadata to events and pageviews for granular segmentation and filtering
features.evidence_quote: Custom properties: attach metadata to events and pageviews

features.name: goals with custom properties
features.description: Narrow goals by up to three custom property values (e.g., purchase for a specific subscription plan)
features.evidence_quote: You can now select up to three existing custom properties and values directly in the goal settings.

features.name: filters and segments
features.description: Drill into any traffic slice; create reusable audience segments applied across dashboard
features.evidence_quote: Filters and segments: drill into any slice of your traffic

features.name: limited dashboard sharing
features.description: Share only a filtered segment of stats; recipients can't remove the base filter
features.evidence_quote: Anyone opening such a shared link will only see that segment and won't be able to remove it.

features.name: shared links
features.description: Share dashboard publicly or with specific people via link
features.evidence_quote: Shared links: share your dashboard publicly or with specific people

features.name: consolidated multi-site view
features.description: Aggregate traffic and key metrics across all sites in one unified dashboard
features.evidence_quote: This gives you an overview of traffic across all your websites...total number of visitors and other key metrics

features.name: site sorting
features.description: Sort multi-site list by most/least visitors (24h) or domain name; pinned sites stay at top
features.evidence_quote: you can now sort them by: Most/least visitors in the last 24 hours, Domain name (A–Z or Z–A)

features.name: compare periods
features.description: Benchmark current stats against previous time ranges
features.evidence_quote: Compare periods: benchmark against previous time ranges

features.name: last 24 hours time range
features.description: Dedicated rolling 24-hour time range in dashboard picker with keyboard shortcut H
features.evidence_quote: A new time range for "Last 24 Hours" is now available on your dashboard.

features.name: percentage column in reports
features.description: Hover to reveal percentage of visitors per source, location, device, and page in expanded reports
features.evidence_quote: You can now see what percentage of your visitors came from different traffic sources, locations, and devices

features.name: traffic spike notifications
features.description: Email alerts when site traffic spikes beyond a threshold
features.evidence_quote: Traffic spike notifications: get alerted when traffic spikes

features.name: email reports
features.description: Scheduled analytics reports delivered to inbox
features.evidence_quote: Email reports: scheduled reports delivered to your inbox

features.name: slack reports
features.description: Send scheduled analytics reports to a Slack channel
features.evidence_quote: Slack reports: send reports to a Slack channel

features.name: sso
features.description: Single sign-on for Google Workspaces, Microsoft Entra, and Okta
features.evidence_quote: Single Sign-On (SSO): for teams on Google Workspaces, Microsoft Entra, or Okta

features.name: team management
features.description: Invite team members, assign roles and permissions, manage agency client sites
features.evidence_quote: Users and roles: invite team members and set permissions

features.name: agency and freelancer management
features.description: Manage multiple client sites from a single account with appropriate access controls
features.evidence_quote: Agencies and freelancers: manage multiple client sites

features.name: scheduled raw event data exports
features.description: Automated scheduled exports of privacy-friendly event-level raw data for enterprise customers
features.evidence_quote: Enterprise customers now have the option to access their privacy-friendly event level data with scheduled raw event data exports.

features.name: google analytics import
features.description: Import historical stats from Google Analytics into Plausible
features.evidence_quote: Import from Google Analytics: bring in your historical data

features.name: csv import
features.description: Import stats from external sources via CSV upload
features.evidence_quote: Import stats: bring in data via CSV

features.name: export stats
features.description: Download analytics data for offline use
features.evidence_quote: Export stats: download your data

features.name: embed dashboard
features.description: Embed Plausible stats dashboard on any external page
features.evidence_quote: Embed dashboard: embed your stats on any page

features.name: looker studio connector
features.description: Connect Plausible data to Google Looker Studio for custom branded reports and blended dashboards
features.evidence_quote: Build custom dashboards, branded client reports and views that blend Plausible data with other sources. Available on the Business plan.

features.name: stats api
features.description: Query analytics data programmatically; includes interactive API playground
features.evidence_quote: Stats API: query your analytics data programmatically

features.name: events api
features.description: Send custom events and pageviews from backend or server-side without JavaScript
features.evidence_quote: Events API: send custom events from your backend or server

features.name: sites api
features.description: Manage sites and goals programmatically via API
features.evidence_quote: Sites API: manage sites and goals via API

features.name: outbound link click tracking
features.description: Automatically track clicks to external sites without custom code
features.evidence_quote: Outbound link clicks: track clicks to external sites

features.name: file download tracking
features.description: Track which files visitors download automatically
features.evidence_quote: File downloads: see which files get downloaded

features.name: form submission tracking
features.description: Track form completions without extra custom code
features.evidence_quote: Form submissions: track form completions without extra setup

features.name: 404 error page tracking
features.description: Detect broken links and missing pages via built-in goal tracking
features.evidence_quote: 404 error pages: catch broken links and missing pages

features.name: bot filtering
features.description: Built-in exclusion of bots, referrer spam, and data center traffic out of the box
features.evidence_quote: Exclude bots, referrer spam and data center traffic so your stats reflect real human visits, out of the box.

features.name: first-party proxy (adblocker bypass)
features.description: Run Plausible script as first-party connection from own domain to bypass adblockers
features.evidence_quote: You can run Plausible script as a first-party connection from your domain name in order to bypass adblockers.

features.name: server-side tracking
features.description: Record pageviews and events server-side without JavaScript via Events API
features.evidence_quote: Our events API can be used to record pageviews and custom events server side without the use of JavaScript.

features.name: mobile app tracking
features.description: Track users on Android and iOS mobile apps via the Events API
features.evidence_quote: You can use the events API to count users on your Android and/or iOS mobile apps too.

features.name: wordpress plugin
features.description: Official plugin with WooCommerce revenue tracking, auto form tracking, author/category stats, and proxy mode
features.evidence_quote: supports WooCommerce revenue tracking, automatic form tracking, author and category stats, proxy mode

features.name: google tag manager template
features.description: Official GTM community template for no-code Plausible installation and event tracking
features.evidence_quote: Install Plausible and configure event tracking from the GTM dashboard with no code changes.

features.name: npm package
features.description: Official npm package for JavaScript framework integrations (React, Vue, Next.js, etc.)
features.evidence_quote: The official NPM package lets you add Plausible to any JavaScript project via your package manager.

features.name: open source and self-hosting
features.description: Fully public, auditable source code; deployable on own infrastructure as alternative to hosted version
features.evidence_quote: The code is public and auditable. Self-host on your own infrastructure or use our hosted version.

features.name: eu data hosting
features.description: All data stored on European-owned infrastructure; never leaves EU jurisdiction
features.evidence_quote: Built and hosted in the EU. Your data never leaves European-owned infrastructure.

features.name: cookieless tracking
features.description: Tracks visitors without cookies; no GDPR/CCPA consent banner needed
features.evidence_quote: No cookies, just insights.

features.name: two-factor authentication
features.description: 2FA for securing user accounts
features.evidence_quote: Two-factor authentication: secure your account

features.name: dashboard appearance settings
features.description: Light mode, dark mode, and other visual appearance customization
features.evidence_quote: Dashboard appearance: light mode, dark mode and more

features.name: excluding pages and traffic
features.description: Filter out internal visits and specific pages from analytics data
features.evidence_quote: Excluding pages and traffic: filter out internal visits

features.name: white-label analytics
features.description: Branded analytics offering for agencies delivering dashboards to clients
features.evidence_quote: White-label analytics

features.name: traffic channels and sources report
features.description: Breakdown of traffic by channel, referrer source, and campaign
features.evidence_quote: Channels, sources and campaigns: where traffic comes from

features.name: nonprofit and education pricing
features.description: Discounted subscription plans for nonprofit and education organizations
features.evidence_quote: Nonprofit and education pricing: discounted plans

features.name: gdpr and ccpa compliance
features.description: Built-in data processing compliance; DPA available; no personal data collection
features.evidence_quote: Compliance: GDPR, CCPA and data processing

features.name: login management
features.description: Review and manage active login sessions
features.evidence_quote: Login management: review active sessions

differentiators: No cookies, no consent banner required — GDPR/CCPA compliant by default without configuration
Script is 54x smaller than Google Analytics (135KB less JS per visitor per page load)
Built and hosted in the EU on exclusively European-owned infrastructure
Single-page dashboard requires zero training or custom report building
Automatic scroll depth tracking with no setup or tag managers
AI referral traffic monitoring (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude) as a built-in report
Built-in bot, referrer spam, and data center traffic filtering out of the box

recent_ships.what: User Journeys — explore actual paths visitors take from start/end points with drop-off and conversion rates
recent_ships.source: You can now see the actual paths visitors take through your site with the new User Journeys feature.

recent_ships.what: Strict order funnels — enforce exact consecutive step ordering; detours disqualify conversion
recent_ships.source: When enabled, every step must happen in exact consecutive order with no other actions in between.

recent_ships.what: Sort sites by traffic — sort multi-site list by visitors in last 24h or domain name
recent_ships.source: you can now sort them by: Most/least visitors in the last 24 hours, Domain name (A–Z or Z–A)

recent_ships.what: Last 24 Hours time range — rolling 24h window in dashboard picker with keyboard shortcut H
recent_ships.source: A new time range for "Last 24 Hours" is now available on your dashboard.

recent_ships.what: Goals with custom properties — narrow goals by up to three custom property values
recent_ships.source: You can now select up to three existing custom properties and values directly in the goal settings.

recent_ships.what: Limited dashboard sharing — share only a filtered segment; recipients cannot remove the base filter
recent_ships.source: When sharing your dashboard with external parties, you can now choose to show only specific, filtered data

recent_ships.what: Scheduled raw event data exports — enterprise-only automated exports of event-level data
recent_ships.source: Enterprise customers now have the option to access their privacy-friendly event level data with scheduled raw event data exports.

recent_ships.what: Revenue breakdown in reports — revenue column per source, page, location, device when revenue goal active
recent_ships.source: you can now see a breakdown of your revenue within the individual reports as well

recent_ships.what: Consolidated Views — single dashboard aggregating traffic across all sites in account
recent_ships.source: This gives you an overview of traffic across all your websites, allows you to quickly see the total number of visitors

praised: Ease of use: 'SOOOO easy to use (for clients too) and the data is near real-time' — Cyrus Shepard, SEO consultant
UX and design quality: 'I can't remember when last I was this impressed by a SaaS UX + design' — Rob Hope, product designer
Feature completeness for practical needs: 'All the conversion tracking we need, it has every report we use.' — Laura Roeder, Paperbell
Reliable at scale: 'Plausible runs reliably at any scale, from small sites to hundreds of millions of monthly visitors'
Quick time to value: 'Became a paying customer within 1hr of the 30-day trial.' — Rob Hope
Enterprise adoption: 'We're massive users of Plausible here at Hugging Face.' — Clem Delangue, CEO Hugging Face
Drop-in GA replacement: 'We switched over everything from GA.' — John O'Nolan, CEO Ghost

tiers.name: Business
tiers.headline_inclusions: Includes Looker Studio connector; pricing page not fully captured in provided sources

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Adds SSO (Google Workspaces, Entra, Okta) and scheduled raw event data exports

pricing.notes: 30-day free trial; no permanent free tier. Pricing appears usage-based on monthly pageviews, not per-seat. Business plan required for Looker Studio connector. Enterprise adds SSO and raw data exports. Nonprofit/education discounts available. Actual tier prices not present in provided source text.
